mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in the majority of periods following declining FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment shocks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, consumption demand is firm but housing demand is not broadening, indicating incomplete cyclical recovery that should support only modest positive equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS is rising month-over-month while HOUST remains below its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and HOUST-weak condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 5-day forward returns are negative on average during triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns do not improve after INDPRO uptrends, or if the signal is null in periods where industrial production is clearly expanding.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in GDPC1 and PAYEMS indicates broad real-demand expansion and stronger labor income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-PAYEMS expansion signal fails to outperform single-series benchmarks or if 60-day returns are not positive in expansion windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated and FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation impulse, real policy stays accommodative and equities should rise over the next month as discount-rate pressure stays muted.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes where inflation exceeds the policy rate trend do not show above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count signals payroll contraction and weaker realized income, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next week as firms revise earnings expectations down.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if the average return after payroll contraction is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound reflected by rising HOUST should translate into stronger cyclical growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, but only when retail sales are also firm.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month average and RSAFS is not declining
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturns without matching retail strength do not produce positive 20-day returns, or if the joint condition underperforms HOUST alone in walk-forward testing.
